column1: Jeremy Pena leads the pack with a red-hot bat, sitting atop the order and primed to cash in on a juicy platoon advantage against Kyle Freeland in a hitter-friendly park—expect plenty of action from this steady leadoff man. Carlos Correa isn't far behind, also drawing Freeland in the same favorable environment, and his middle-of-the-order spot means more RBI chances and solid protection. Trea Turner and Jose Altuve round out the top options, both boasting reliable profiles and strong matchups, while Alec Burleson offers sneaky value with his lefty stick in a plus spot. Ride the top of this list for your best shot at keeping the streak alive.
